findings.reason: Pattern 1: the contrast scaffolding can be cut and the identifying noun still lands cleanly.

findings.line: No helm. No hammer. Just a man too broad for the furniture and too tired for pretense.
findings.deletionTest: Just a man too broad for the furniture and too tired for pretense.
findings.reason: Pattern 8: the double refusal is reveal scaffolding around the real image.
findings.suggestedFix: Bareheaded and unarmed, he was just a man too broad for the furniture and too tired for pretense.

findings.line: Nothing. Not even the right-corner shadow of a smile.
findings.reason: Pattern 2: the second fragment uses negation as a dramatic afterbeat instead of stating the blank reaction directly.

audits.secondSweep: Completed the dedicated pass-A P5/P6 re-read across the full chapter. No additional pass-A kills survived beyond the findings below.

findings.line: The chains made less noise in chairs and meant the same thing.
findings.subject: chains
findings.realAgent: The restraints and guarded seating still signaled imprisonment.
findings.reason: Pattern 5: the sentence launders the institutional meaning of the restraints onto the chains themselves.
findings.suggestedFix: The chains made less noise in chairs. They were still restraints.

findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: The people in the chamber tensed around Uther's answer.
findings.reason: Pattern 7: the pressure belongs to the crowd's reaction, not to the room itself.
findings.suggestedFix: People all through the room went still.

findings.line: The politics of the city made martyrs expensive.
findings.subject: politics of the city
findings.realAgent: The city's rulers and legal managers treated martyrdom as politically costly.
findings.reason: Pattern 4/5 hybrid: an institutional abstraction is doing the human cost calculation instead of the people running the sentence.
findings.suggestedFix: The city's rulers found martyrs expensive.

findings.line: Nothing. Not even the right-corner shadow of a smile.
findings.reason: This is the second corner-of-mouth near-smile in the chapter, so it exceeds the hard cap of one and is the weaker occurrence to cut.

summary.verdictReason: Seven mild pass-A kills remain after revision, which exceeds the pass threshold of three mild findings.
